title: Open wCommand Screen And View Command Word Values For Each Tipper
summary: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen, use the WCOMMAND button to access the wCommand screen, and review the displayed input and output Command Word values for each tipper side. Use NEXT SIDE to switch between left and right tipper views and observe the current communication shown between the WCS and the operator station.
when_to_use: Use when L1 support needs to view the current input and output Command Word values for a tipper from the operator station HMI and verify the communication display between the WCS and the operator station.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to interpret the meaning of specific Command Word values, because the source only supports viewing the values and does not provide interpretation rules.
Do not use this procedure as a troubleshooting decision tree or corrective action procedure.

responsibilities: Open the wCommand screen using WCOMMAND.
Navigate between left and right tipper views using NEXT SIDE.
Observe and record the displayed Command Word values exactly as shown.

safety_notes: This is a diagnostic viewing procedure based on operator station HMI screens.
The source does not provide any lockout, production stop, or physical intervention requirements for this viewing task.

access_required: Access to the Operator Station HMI
system_preconditions: VISU_MANCONTROL must be available on the operator station HMI.
The user must be able to access the maintenance-oriented manual control screen view referenced by the source.
external_references: Figure 4-23 Operator Station HMI Manual Control Screen

visual_references.description: VISU_MANCONTROL manual control screen showing the WCOMMAND button and maintenance-user screen layout.
wCommand screen showing the command value display referenced by the procedure.
Operator station display controls used to navigate to HMI screens including Visu_ManControl.

steps.title: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen
steps.purpose: Reach the manual control screen that provides access to the wCommand screen.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_MANCONTROL screen on the Operator Station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_MANCONTROL screen is displayed on the operator station.
steps.healthy_condition: The manual control screen is visible and includes the maintenance-user controls and access buttons described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: VISU_MANCONTROL cannot be opened or the expected manual control screen is not displayed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen-navigation controls and mapping showing access to Visu_ManControl.
Overall VISU_MANCONTROL screen layout for maintenance users.

stop_or_escalate_if: VISU_MANCONTROL cannot be accessed on the operator station HMI.
The displayed screen does not match the documented manual control screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: VISU_MANCONTROL screen on the Operator Station HMI is the manual control screen shown for maintenance users.
The listed control mapping includes F1 to "Visu_ManControl."
The screen shown is what personnel logged in as maintenance will see; Figure 4-23 is the Operator Station HMI Manual Control Screen.

steps.title: Press WCOMMAND to open the wCommand screen
steps.purpose: Open the screen that displays the input and output Command Word values for the respective tipper.
steps.instruction: On the VISU_MANCONTROL screen, locate and press the WCOMMAND button to open the wCommand screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The wCommand screen is displayed after pressing WCOMMAND.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing WCOMMAND does not open the wCommand screen.

actions.label: Open wCommand
actions.exact_action: Locate and press the WCOMMAND button to open the wCommand screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The WCOMMAND button on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen.
The resulting wCommand screen after WCOMMAND is pressed.

stop_or_escalate_if: The WCOMMAND button does not open the wCommand screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The WCOMMAND button opens a screen with the input and output Command Word values for the respective tipper to WCS.
The wCommand screen is opened from VISU_MANCONTROL using the WCOMMAND button.

steps.title: Review the displayed Command Word values for the current tipper side
steps.purpose: Inspect the currently displayed input and output Command Word values for the selected tipper.
steps.instruction: Review the input and output Command Word values shown on the wCommand screen for the respective tipper.
steps.expected_result: Input and output Command Word values are visible for the currently displayed tipper side.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen shows the input and output Command Word values for the respective tipper.
steps.failure_condition: Command Word values are not displayed on the wCommand screen.

actions.label: Inspect current command values
actions.exact_action: Review the input and output Command Word values shown for the respective tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The command value display area showing input and output Command Word values.

stop_or_escalate_if: Command Word values are not displayed when the screen is opened.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The WCOMMAND button opens a screen with the input and output Command Word values for the respective tipper to WCS.

steps.title: Use NEXT SIDE to switch between left and right tipper views
steps.purpose: Navigate between the two tipper-side views available on the wCommand screen.
steps.instruction: Press the NEXT SIDE button to navigate between the left and right tipper views.
steps.expected_result: The displayed tipper side changes between left and right.
steps.healthy_condition: NEXT SIDE changes the view between the left and right tipper as documented.
steps.failure_condition: NEXT SIDE does not navigate between left and right tipper views.

actions.label: Switch tipper side
actions.exact_action: Press the NEXT SIDE button to navigate between the left and right tipper views.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The wCommand screen while switching between left and right tipper views.
Reference manual control screen context for access path to wCommand.

stop_or_escalate_if: NEXT SIDE does not navigate between left and right tipper views as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To navigate between the left and right tipper, press the NEXT SIDE button.
NEXT SIDE navigates between left and right tipper.

steps.title: Observe Command Word values on each side
steps.purpose: View the communication values shown for each tipper side between the WCS and the operator station.
steps.instruction: On each side, observe the displayed Command Word values that represent the current communication between the WCS and the operator station.
steps.expected_result: The current communication values are visible for each viewed tipper side.
steps.healthy_condition: For each selected side, the wCommand screen displays the current communication between the WCS and the operator station.
steps.failure_condition: The communication values are missing or not visible for one or both sides.

actions.label: Observe side-specific communication values
actions.exact_action: On each side, observe the displayed Command Word values that represent the current communication between the WCS and the operator station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Displayed input and output Command Word values for the currently selected left or right tipper side.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed values are not visible for a selected side.
The screen does not show the current communication display described by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This displays the current communication between the WCS and the operator station.
It shows input and output Command Word values for the respective tipper to WCS and displays current communication between WCS and the operator station.

steps.title: Record the displayed values exactly as shown
steps.purpose: Preserve the observed Command Word values for the viewed side or sides.
steps.instruction: Record the displayed values for the viewed side or sides exactly as shown.
steps.expected_result: The observed Command Word values are documented exactly as displayed on the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The values for each viewed side are captured without alteration.
steps.failure_condition: The values cannot be recorded or the displayed values are not available to document.

actions.label: Document displayed values
actions.exact_action: Record the displayed values for the viewed side or sides exactly as shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The exact displayed Command Word values to be recorded.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed values are not available to record.
The screen does not show values for the viewed side or sides.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The WCOMMAND button opens a screen with the input and output Command Word values for the respective tipper to WCS.
Record the displayed values for the viewed side or sides exactly as shown.

success_criteria: The wCommand screen opens from VISU_MANCONTROL using WCOMMAND.
The user can navigate between left and right tipper views using NEXT SIDE.
Input and output Command Word values are visible for the viewed tipper side or sides.
The displayed values are recorded exactly as shown.
healthy_conditions: VISU_MANCONTROL is accessible on the operator station HMI.
NEXT SIDE switches between left and right tipper views.
The wCommand screen displays current communication values between the WCS and the operator station.
failure_conditions: The WCOMMAND button does not open the wCommand screen.
NEXT SIDE does not navigate between left and right tipper views as documented.
Command Word values are not displayed when the screen is opened.
The expected communication values are not visible for one or both sides.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the WCOMMAND button does not open the wCommand screen.
Escalate if NEXT SIDE does not navigate between left and right tipper views as documented.
Escalate if Command Word values are not displayed when the screen is opened.
Escalate if the screen shown does not match the documented VISU_MANCONTROL or wCommand screen references.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: WCOMMAND button on the VISU_MANCONTROL screen and the maintenance-user manual control layout.
wCommand screen and the displayed input/output Command Word values.
Navigation controls showing access to Visu_ManControl.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The WCOMMAND button opens a screen with the input and output Command Word values for the respective tipper to WCS. To navigate between the left and right tipper, press the NEXT SIDE button. This displays the current communication between the WCS and the operator station.

missing_details: The source does not provide interpretation rules for specific Command Word values.
The source does not define expected numeric or bit-pattern values for healthy versus faulty communication.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this procedure.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this viewing task.
The source does not specify any command-line, API, or service commands for this procedure.
